codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of the HistoryPage feature. All required behaviors are implemented: audit list with status badges (correctly colored), status filter tabs, URL search input, clickable rows navigating to report, rerun button with same params, compare checkbox selection with 2-audit gate, route in App.tsx, navigation in Layout.tsx, getAudits() in API client, AuditListParams in types.ts, glass-morphism styling, and 15 comprehensive tests. Component uses good React patterns: useEffect cleanup with cancelled flag, useMemo for filtered results, proper error/loading states, stopPropagation on nested interactive elements, and accessibility attributes (role='tab', role='button', tabIndex, aria-labels, keyboard handlers).

issues.description: AuditListParams interface is defined but never imported or used anywhere. The getAudits() function in client.ts defines its params inline as { status?: string; url_contains?: string } instead of referencing this type. Dead code.
Layout mode is hardcoded as mode='live'. The history page shows audits of all modes, so hardcoding 'live' is misleading. Consider omitting mode or deriving it contextually.

.search-input has background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.94) which is nearly opaque white. On the dark glass-morphism theme this creates a jarring bright input field. Should use a translucent dark-theme-consistent background like other inputs.

Test 'displays status badges with correct colors' only verifies text content is rendered, not that the correct CSS classes (status-completed, status-failed, etc.) are applied. The test name overpromises what it actually validates.

handleCompare uses Array.from(selectedIds) to destructure [a, b]. While JS Set preserves insertion order, the compare URL parameter ordering depends entirely on user click sequence rather than a deterministic sort. Consider sorting IDs for consistent URLs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker added AuditListParams type to types.ts but the getAudits function in client.ts defines params inline instead of using the type. This creates an unused type definition — a minor pattern inconsistency. AGENTS.md doesn't specify whether API client functions should reference types.ts interfaces for their parameters.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: frontend/src/api/types.ts:114-117 defines AuditListParams; frontend/src/api/client.ts:36 uses inline { status?: string; url_contains?: string } instead

sharedStateObservations.observation: The component hardcodes mode='live' in the Layout wrapper even though the page displays audits of all modes. Other pages (SubmitPage) don't pass mode to Layout, or derive it from the audit data. No convention exists in AGENTS.md about when/how to set the mode prop on Layout.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: frontend/src/pages/HistoryPage.tsx:141 — <Layout mode='live'> vs frontend/src/pages/SubmitPage.tsx which uses a readiness check to determine mode

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill procedure was followed correctly. Worker performed TDD (wrote tests first, verified they fail, then implemented), ran all 4 verification commands (test, lint, typecheck, build), and committed with descriptive message. No deviations reported and skillFeedback confirms followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff verification shows all 4 commands with exitCode 0. Transcript shows test file created before component file.

summary: The history-page feature is well-implemented with all required behaviors present: audit list display, colored status badges, status filter tabs, URL search, clickable rows for navigation, rerun functionality, and compare selection with 2-audit gate. Tests are comprehensive (15 cases covering loading, error, rendering, filtering, navigation, rerun, compare logic). Five non-blocking issues were found: unused AuditListParams type, hardcoded Layout mode, bright search input background inconsistent with dark theme, test name overpromising color verification, and non-deterministic compare URL parameter ordering. None of these issues block the feature or affect functionality. Passing.
